1→# Prompt 020: Recurrence - Natural Language & Template Integration (P2)
     2→
     3→## Objective
     4→Add natural language recurrence parser and template integration to recurring tasks, matching Todoist/ClickUp UX.
     5→
     6→## Context
     7→- **Feature:** Recurring Tasks (P2 - Natural language, Template integration)
     8→- **Current State:** Full recurrence engine with all frequency patterns, recur-from-completion
     9→- **P2 Gap:** No natural language input, recurring tasks don't use templates
    10→- **Priority:** P2 UX Enhancement - Faster setup and reduced repetitive work
    11→
    12→## Requirements
    13→
    14→### 1. Natural Language Input
    15→**Purpose:** Parse human-readable recurrence descriptions (e.g., "every 2 weeks on Monday")
    16→
    17→**Supported Phrases:**
    18→- **Daily:** "every day", "every 3 days", "daily", "every weekday"
    19→- **Weekly:** "every week", "every Monday", "every 2 weeks on Friday", "weekly"
    20→- **Monthly:** "every month", "every 15th", "every last Friday", "monthly on the 10th"
    21→- **Yearly:** "every year", "annually", "every January 15th"
    22→
    23→**Examples:**
    24→| Input | Parsed To |
    25→|-------|-----------|
    26→| "every day" | Daily, interval 1 |
    27→| "every 3 days" | Daily, interval 3 |
    28→| "every weekday" | Weekly, Mon-Fri |
    29→| "every Monday" | Weekly, Monday |
    30→| "every 2 weeks on Friday" | Weekly, interval 2, Friday |
    31→| "every 15th" | Monthly, day 15 |
    32→| "every last Friday" | Monthly, last Friday |
    33→| "every January 15th" | Yearly, Jan 15 |
    34→
    35→**UI:**
    36→- Text input above RecurrencePicker: "Quick Setup (optional)"
    37→- Placeholder: "e.g., every 2 weeks on Monday"
    38→- Parse on blur/Enter key
    39→- If parsed successfully → auto-fill RecurrencePicker fields
    40→- If parse fails → show error + keep existing values
    41→- Clear button to reset
    42→
    43→**Implementation:**
    44→- Create `parseRecurrenceNaturalLanguage(input: string): RecurrenceRule | null`
    45→- Regex patterns for each frequency type
    46→- Handle intervals (numbers), day names, month names
    47→- Case-insensitive matching
    48→- Return null if unparseable (don't throw errors)
    49→
    50→### 2. Template Integration
    51→**Purpose:** Pre-fill recurring task instances with checklist items, description, custom fields
    52→
    53→**Use Cases:**
    54→- Weekly team meeting (same agenda checklist every week)
    55→- Monthly report (same subtasks, same description template)
    56→- Daily standup (same questions in checklist)
    57→
    58→**Implementation:**
    59→1. **Task Template System** (if doesn't exist yet)
    60→   - Add `isTemplate: boolean` to Task type
    61→   - Add "Save as Template" button in CardDetailPanel
    62→   - Template library in Settings or Kanban menu
    63→   - Templates: title, description, checklist, labels, customFields (NOT due date, assignee, status)
    64→
    65→2. **Recurring Task → Template Link**
    66→   - Add `templateId?: string` to RecurrenceRule interface
    67→   - In RecurrencePicker: "Use Template" dropdown (optional)
    68→   - When creating next occurrence:
    69→     - Copy all template fields to new instance
    70→     - Override with recurrence-specific fields (due date, title suffix)
    71→     - Keep instance relationship (recurrenceId)
    72→
    73→3. **Template Picker UI**
    74→   - In RecurrencePicker: Collapsible "Advanced" section
    75→   - Dropdown: "Use Template (optional)"
    76→   - Lists all saved templates
    77→   - Preview template content on hover/select
    78→   - "Create Template" shortcut button
    79→
    80→**Edge Cases:**
    81→- Template deleted → recurring task continues without template
    82→- Template modified → future instances use new template version
    83→- Manual edits to instance → don't propagate to template
    84→- Recurrence settings changed → doesn't affect template
    85→
    86→## Files to Create
    87→
    88→### Utils
    89→**`src/utils/naturalLanguageRecurrence.ts`**
    90→```typescript
    91→export function parseRecurrenceNaturalLanguage(
    92→  input: string
    93→): RecurrenceRule | null
    94→
    95→// Helper functions
    96→function parseDailyPattern(input: string): RecurrenceRule | null
    97→function parseWeeklyPattern(input: string): RecurrenceRule | null
    98→function parseMonthlyPattern(input: string): RecurrenceRule | null
    99→function parseYearlyPattern(input: string): RecurrenceRule | null
   100→```
   101→
   102→**`src/services/taskTemplates.ts`** (if needed)
   103→```typescript
   104→export interface TaskTemplate {
   105→  id: string;
   106→  name: string;
   107→  description: string;
   108→  checklist: ChecklistItem[];
   109→  labels: string[];
   110→  customFields: Record<string, unknown>;
   111→  createdAt: Date;
   112→}
   113→
   114→export function saveTaskAsTemplate(task: Task): TaskTemplate
   115→export function applyTemplate(templateId: string, task: Partial<Task>): Task
   116→```
   117→
   118→### Components
   119→**`src/components/TemplatePicker.tsx`**
   120→- Dropdown list of templates
   121→- Search/filter templates
   122→- Preview template content
   123→- "Create Template" button
   124→- Used in RecurrencePicker
   125→
   126→## Files to Modify
   127→
   128→### Types
   129→**`src/types/index.ts`**
   130→- Add `isTemplate?: boolean` to Task interface
   131→- Add `templateId?: string` to RecurrenceRule interface
   132→- Add TaskTemplate interface (or in separate file)
   133→
   134→### Components
   135→**`src/components/RecurrencePicker.tsx`**
   136→- Add natural language input field at top
   137→- Wire up parser to auto-fill fields
   138→- Add "Advanced" collapsible section
   139→- Add template picker dropdown
   140→- Show selected template preview
   141→
   142→**`src/widgets/Kanban/CardDetailPanel.tsx`**
   143→- Add "Save as Template" button
   144→- Wire up template creation
   145→- Show template indicator if task is a template
   146→
   147→### Services
   148→**`src/services/taskRecurrence.ts`**
   149→- Update `generateNextOccurrence()` to apply template if `templateId` set
   150→- Merge template fields with recurrence overrides
   151→- Handle missing/deleted templates gracefully
   152→
   153→### Stores
   154→**`src/stores/useKanbanStore.ts`** (or new `useTemplateStore.ts`)
   155→- Add template CRUD actions (create, read, update, delete)
   156→- Persist templates to IndexedDB
   157→- Add `templates: TaskTemplate[]` to state
   158→
   159→## Implementation Approach
   160→
   161→### Phase 1: Natural Language Parser
   162→1. Create `naturalLanguageRecurrence.ts` utility
   163→2. Implement regex patterns for each frequency
   164→3. Write comprehensive unit tests (50+ test cases)
   165→4. Handle edge cases (invalid input, ambiguous phrases)
   166→
   167→### Phase 2: UI Integration
   168→1. Add natural language input to RecurrencePicker
   169→2. Wire up parser to auto-fill fields on blur/Enter
   170→3. Show error message if parse fails
   171→4. Add clear button to reset input
   172→
   173→### Phase 3: Template System
   174→1. Add `isTemplate` field to Task type
   175→2. Create template CRUD actions in store
   176→3. Persist templates to IndexedDB
   177→4. Add "Save as Template" button in CardDetailPanel
   178→
   179→### Phase 4: Template Integration
   180→1. Add `templateId` to RecurrenceRule
   181→2. Create TemplatePicker component
   182→3. Add template picker to RecurrencePicker
   183→4. Update `generateNextOccurrence()` to apply templates
   184→5. Handle template deletion/modification
   185→
   186→## Verification
   187→1. Open RecurrencePicker in CardDetailPanel
   188→2. Enter "every 2 weeks on Monday" → verify fields auto-filled
   189→3. Enter "every 15th" → verify monthly recurrence on day 15
   190→4. Enter "invalid text" → verify error message
   191→5. Create task with checklist + description
   192→6. Click "Save as Template" → template saved
   193→7. Create new recurring task
   194→8. Select template in RecurrencePicker
   195→9. Complete current instance → next instance created with template content
   196→10. Verify next instance has:
   197→    - Same checklist items (unchecked)
   198→    - Same description
   199→    - Correct due date from recurrence
   200→11. Test edge cases:
   201→    - Delete template → recurring task continues
   202→    - Modify template → future instances use new version
   203→    - Manual edit instance → doesn't affect template
   204→
   205→## Success Criteria
   206→- ✅ Natural language parser handles 20+ phrase patterns
   207→- ✅ Parser auto-fills RecurrencePicker fields
   208→- ✅ Parse errors shown with clear messaging
   209→- ✅ Templates can be created from tasks
   210→- ✅ Templates stored in IndexedDB
   211→- ✅ Template picker integrated in RecurrencePicker
   212→- ✅ Recurring instances use template content
   213→- ✅ Template deletion doesn't break recurrence
   214→- ✅ UI components follow semantic token patterns
   215→- ✅ Type safety maintained throughout
   216→
   217→## Non-negotiables
   218→- Use Tailwind semantic tokens (no hardcoded colors)
   219→- TypeScript strict mode compliance
   220→- Parser must not throw errors (return null on failure)
   221→- Templates must not include due date or assignee (recurrence-specific)
   222→- Follow CLAUDE.md patterns (stores, components, types)
   223→
   224→## Related Files
   225→- `src/components/RecurrencePicker.tsx` - Recurrence UI
   226→- `src/services/taskRecurrence.ts` - Recurrence logic
   227→- `src/stores/useKanbanStore.ts` - Task store
   228→- `src/types/index.ts` - Task types
   229→
   230→## Estimated Complexity
   231→**Medium-High** - Natural language parser requires extensive testing. Template system is moderate complexity with store integration and UI work. Both can be implemented independently.
   232→

file.content:
# Prompt 020: Recurrence - Natural Language & Template Integration (P2)

## Objective
Add natural language recurrence parser and template integration to recurring tasks, matching Todoist/ClickUp UX.

## Context
- **Feature:** Recurring Tasks (P2 - Natural language, Template integration)
- **Current State:** Full recurrence engine with all frequency patterns, recur-from-completion
- **P2 Gap:** No natural language input, recurring tasks don't use templates
- **Priority:** P2 UX Enhancement - Faster setup and reduced repetitive work

## Requirements

### 1. Natural Language Input
**Purpose:** Parse human-readable recurrence descriptions (e.g., "every 2 weeks on Monday")

**Supported Phrases:**
- **Daily:** "every day", "every 3 days", "daily", "every weekday"
- **Weekly:** "every week", "every Monday", "every 2 weeks on Friday", "weekly"
- **Monthly:** "every month", "every 15th", "every last Friday", "monthly on the 10th"
- **Yearly:** "every year", "annually", "every January 15th"

**Examples:**
| Input | Parsed To |
|-------|-----------|
| "every day" | Daily, interval 1 |
| "every 3 days" | Daily, interval 3 |
| "every weekday" | Weekly, Mon-Fri |
| "every Monday" | Weekly, Monday |
| "every 2 weeks on Friday" | Weekly, interval 2, Friday |
| "every 15th" | Monthly, day 15 |
| "every last Friday" | Monthly, last Friday |
| "every January 15th" | Yearly, Jan 15 |

**UI:**
- Text input above RecurrencePicker: "Quick Setup (optional)"
- Placeholder: "e.g., every 2 weeks on Monday"
- Parse on blur/Enter key
- If parsed successfully → auto-fill RecurrencePicker fields
- If parse fails → show error + keep existing values
- Clear button to reset

**Implementation:**
- Create `parseRecurrenceNaturalLanguage(input: string): RecurrenceRule | null`
- Regex patterns for each frequency type
- Handle intervals (numbers), day names, month names
- Case-insensitive matching
- Return null if unparseable (don't throw errors)

### 2. Template Integration
**Purpose:** Pre-fill recurring task instances with checklist items, description, custom fields

**Use Cases:**
- Weekly team meeting (same agenda checklist every week)
- Monthly report (same subtasks, same description template)
- Daily standup (same questions in checklist)

**Implementation:**
1. **Task Template System** (if doesn't exist yet)
   - Add `isTemplate: boolean` to Task type
   - Add "Save as Template" button in CardDetailPanel
   - Template library in Settings or Kanban menu
   - Templates: title, description, checklist, labels, customFields (NOT due date, assignee, status)

2. **Recurring Task → Template Link**
   - Add `templateId?: string` to RecurrenceRule interface
   - In RecurrencePicker: "Use Template" dropdown (optional)
   - When creating next occurrence:
     - Copy all template fields to new instance
     - Override with recurrence-specific fields (due date, title suffix)
     - Keep instance relationship (recurrenceId)

3. **Template Picker UI**
   - In RecurrencePicker: Collapsible "Advanced" section
   - Dropdown: "Use Template (optional)"
   - Lists all saved templates
   - Preview template content on hover/select
   - "Create Template" shortcut button

**Edge Cases:**
- Template deleted → recurring task continues without template
- Template modified → future instances use new template version
- Manual edits to instance → don't propagate to template
- Recurrence settings changed → doesn't affect template

## Files to Create

### Utils
**`src/utils/naturalLanguageRecurrence.ts`**
```typescript
export function parseRecurrenceNaturalLanguage(
  input: string
): RecurrenceRule | null

// Helper functions
function parseDailyPattern(input: string): RecurrenceRule | null
function parseWeeklyPattern(input: string): RecurrenceRule | null
function parseMonthlyPattern(input: string): RecurrenceRule | null
function parseYearlyPattern(input: string): RecurrenceRule | null
```

**`src/services/taskTemplates.ts`** (if needed)
```typescript
export interface TaskTemplate {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description: string;
  checklist: ChecklistItem[];
  labels: string[];
  customFields: Record<string, unknown>;
  createdAt: Date;
}

export function saveTaskAsTemplate(task: Task): TaskTemplate
export function applyTemplate(templateId: string, task: Partial<Task>): Task
```

### Components
**`src/components/TemplatePicker.tsx`**
- Dropdown list of templates
- Search/filter templates
- Preview template content
- "Create Template" button
- Used in RecurrencePicker

## Files to Modify

### Types
**`src/types/index.ts`**
- Add `isTemplate?: boolean` to Task interface
- Add `templateId?: string` to RecurrenceRule interface
- Add TaskTemplate interface (or in separate file)

### Components
**`src/components/RecurrencePicker.tsx`**
- Add natural language input field at top
- Wire up parser to auto-fill fields
- Add "Advanced" collapsible section
- Add template picker dropdown
- Show selected template preview

**`src/widgets/Kanban/CardDetailPanel.tsx`**
- Add "Save as Template" button
- Wire up template creation
- Show template indicator if task is a template

### Services
**`src/services/taskRecurrence.ts`**
- Update `generateNextOccurrence()` to apply template if `templateId` set
- Merge template fields with recurrence overrides
- Handle missing/deleted templates gracefully

### Stores
**`src/stores/useKanbanStore.ts`** (or new `useTemplateStore.ts`)
- Add template CRUD actions (create, read, update, delete)
- Persist templates to IndexedDB
- Add `templates: TaskTemplate[]` to state

## Implementation Approach

### Phase 1: Natural Language Parser
1. Create `naturalLanguageRecurrence.ts` utility
2. Implement regex patterns for each frequency
3. Write comprehensive unit tests (50+ test cases)
4. Handle edge cases (invalid input, ambiguous phrases)

### Phase 2: UI Integration
1. Add natural language input to RecurrencePicker
2. Wire up parser to auto-fill fields on blur/Enter
3. Show error message if parse fails
4. Add clear button to reset input

### Phase 3: Template System
1. Add `isTemplate` field to Task type
2. Create template CRUD actions in store
3. Persist templates to IndexedDB
4. Add "Save as Template" button in CardDetailPanel

### Phase 4: Template Integration
1. Add `templateId` to RecurrenceRule
2. Create TemplatePicker component
3. Add template picker to RecurrencePicker
4. Update `generateNextOccurrence()` to apply templates
5. Handle template deletion/modification

## Verification
1. Open RecurrencePicker in CardDetailPanel
2. Enter "every 2 weeks on Monday" → verify fields auto-filled
3. Enter "every 15th" → verify monthly recurrence on day 15
4. Enter "invalid text" → verify error message
5. Create task with checklist + description
6. Click "Save as Template" → template saved
7. Create new recurring task
8. Select template in RecurrencePicker
9. Complete current instance → next instance created with template content
10. Verify next instance has:
    - Same checklist items (unchecked)
    - Same description
    - Correct due date from recurrence
11. Test edge cases:
    - Delete template → recurring task continues
    - Modify template → future instances use new version
    - Manual edit instance → doesn't affect template

## Success Criteria
- ✅ Natural language parser handles 20+ phrase patterns
- ✅ Parser auto-fills RecurrencePicker fields
- ✅ Parse errors shown with clear messaging
- ✅ Templates can be created from tasks
- ✅ Templates stored in IndexedDB
- ✅ Template picker integrated in RecurrencePicker
- ✅ Recurring instances use template content
- ✅ Template deletion doesn't break recurrence
- ✅ UI components follow semantic token patterns
- ✅ Type safety maintained throughout

## Non-negotiables
- Use Tailwind semantic tokens (no hardcoded colors)
- TypeScript strict mode compliance
- Parser must not throw errors (return null on failure)
- Templates must not include due date or assignee (recurrence-specific)
- Follow CLAUDE.md patterns (stores, components, types)

## Related Files
- `src/components/RecurrencePicker.tsx` - Recurrence UI
- `src/services/taskRecurrence.ts` - Recurrence logic
- `src/stores/useKanbanStore.ts` - Task store
- `src/types/index.ts` - Task types

## Estimated Complexity
**Medium-High** - Natural language parser requires extensive testing. Template system is moderate complexity with store integration and UI work. Both can be implemented independently.

240→  cardNumber?: number;             // Auto-incrementing card number (KAN-1, KAN-2, etc.)
   241→
   242→  // PHASE B (KANBAN PM) FIELDS:
   243→  effort?: EffortEstimate;         // Story points (1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13)
   244→  customStatus?: CustomStatus;     // Additional status labels (in-review, testing, deployed, blocked)
   245→  timeTracking?: TimeTracking;     // Time tracking with history
   246→
   247→  // RECURRING TASKS FIELDS (P1):
   248→  recurrence?: {
   249→    frequency: 'daily' | 'weekly' | 'monthly' | 'yearly';
   250→    interval: number; // e.g., every 2 weeks
   251→    daysOfWeek?: number[]; // 0=Sun, 1=Mon, etc. (for weekly)
   252→    dayOfMonth?: number; // 1-31 (for monthly)
   253→    endType: 'never' | 'after' | 'until';
   254→    endCount?: number; // if endType='after'
   255→    endDate?: string; // if endType='until' (YYYY-MM-DD)
   256→    recurFromCompletion?: boolean; // If true, recur from completion date instead of due date (default: false)
   257→  };

\u26a0\ufe0f Security Warning: Using child_process.exec() can lead to command injection vulnerabilities.

This codebase provides a safer alternative: src/utils/execFileNoThrow.ts

Instead of:
  exec(`command ${userInput}`)

Use:
  import { execFileNoThrow } from '../utils/execFileNoThrow.js'
  await execFileNoThrow('command', [userInput])

The execFileNoThrow utility:
- Uses execFile instead of exec (prevents shell injection)
- Handles Windows compatibility automatically
- Provides proper error handling
- Returns structured output with stdout, stderr, and status

Only use exec() if you absolutely need shell features and the input is guaranteed to be safe.
